metadata.title: WHO Procedure for Assessing the Acceptability, in Principle, of Vaccines for Purchase by United Nations Agencies — Annex 6
metadata.document_version: WHO Technical Report Series No. 978 (61st Report of WHO Expert Committee on Biological Standardization)

rules.rule_text: Vaccines must be on the WHO priority list before an application for prequalification can be submitted; mandatory programmatic characteristics per the PSPQ document must be met.
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccines must be on the WHO priority list before an application can be submitted. Mandatory programmatic characteristics per the PSPQ document must be met.

rules.rule_text: The relevant national regulatory authority (NRA) must be assessed as functional before a vaccine can be accepted for prequalification; marketing authorization must have been granted by the relevant NRA or through the EMA centralized procedure.
rules.verbatim_quote: The relevant NRA must be assessed as functional. Marketing authorization must have been granted by the relevant NRA or through the EMA centralized procedure.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturers must inform the relevant NRA of their intent to submit an application to WHO prequalification before submitting the application.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers should inform the relevant NRA of their intent to submit an application to WHO prequalification before submitting.

rules.rule_text: The official application letter must be addressed to the Coordinator of Quality Safety Standards at WHO, with copies sent to the vaccines prequalification manager and the relevant NRA.
rules.verbatim_quote: The application letter should be addressed to the Coordinator of Quality Safety Standards at WHO, with copies to the vaccines prequalification manager and the relevant NRA.

rules.rule_text: The Product Summary File (PSF) must be submitted as one hard copy plus five electronic copies on CD-ROM in Microsoft Word or PDF format using the WHO 10-chapter format; CTD format is acceptable if cross-referenced to the WHO PSF chapter structure.
rules.verbatim_quote: The PSF should be submitted as one hard copy plus five electronic copies on CD-ROM in Microsoft Word or PDF format using the WHO 10-chapter format. CTD format is acceptable if cross-referenced.
rules.exceptions: CTD format acceptable with cross-referencing to WHO PSF chapter structure

rules.rule_text: PSF submission deadlines are three times per year: 31 January, 31 May, and 30 September; WHO will screen submissions within 1 month of the deadline for acceptance or rejection.
rules.verbatim_quote: PSF submission deadlines are 31 January, 31 May, and 30 September. WHO will screen submissions within 1 month of the deadline for acceptance or rejection.

rules.rule_text: The time frame for WHO initial review of an accepted PSF is 3 months; a consolidated report will be provided to the manufacturer; complementary information requested must be submitted in a single package.
rules.verbatim_quote: The time frame for initial PSF review is 3 months. A consolidated report will be provided. Complementary information should be submitted in a single package.

rules.rule_text: Initial testing of vaccine samples requires 25–200 samples depending on vaccine type, from 3–5 final lots; samples must be accompanied by lot summary protocols and detailed SOPs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Initial testing requires 25–200 samples depending on vaccine type, from 3–5 final lots. Samples should be accompanied by lot summary protocols and detailed SOPs.
rules.exceptions: Number of samples varies by vaccine type

rules.rule_text: Potency and toxicity are the standard tests performed by WHO on submitted vaccine samples; the time frame from receipt by WHO to completion of testing is 3 months.
rules.verbatim_quote: Potency and toxicity are the standard tests performed by WHO on submitted samples. The time frame from receipt by WHO is 3 months.
rules.exceptions: Additional tests may be required depending on vaccine type

rules.rule_text: WHO site audits must assess GMP compliance, production consistency, quality system, labelling/packaging, vaccine vial monitors (VVM), and the stability programme; site audits are required for new product prequalification and periodic follow-up.
rules.verbatim_quote: WHO site audits should assess GMP compliance, production consistency, quality system, labelling/packaging, VVM, and the stability programme. Audits are required for new product prequalification and periodic follow-up.

rules.rule_text: Site audits are usually conducted within 2 months of satisfactory WHO testing results; the WHO team leads the audit with NRA observers; a bilateral consultation meeting with the NRA is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Site audits are usually conducted within 2 months of satisfactory test results. The WHO team leads with NRA observers. A bilateral consultation meeting with the NRA is required.

rules.rule_text: The final WHO site audit report must be issued within 30 days of the audit; non-compliance findings result in postponement of the final prequalification recommendation until corrective actions are completed.
rules.verbatim_quote: The final audit report should be issued within 30 days. Non-compliance results in postponement of the final recommendation until corrections are completed.

rules.rule_text: Following a positive prequalification decision, a letter is sent to relevant UN agencies and GAVI (for AMC products); prequalification status remains valid until reassessment; WHO may revoke prequalification for fraud or non-compliance.
rules.verbatim_quote: A letter is sent to UN agencies and GAVI for AMC products following a positive decision. Prequalification status is valid until reassessment. WHO may revoke for fraud or non-compliance.

rules.rule_text: Under the streamlined procedure, WHO must review NRA assessment reports instead of the full PSF; WHO must still independently assess: mandatory programmatic characteristics, AMC eligibility, WHO recommendations compliance, stability data for developing countries, clinical data for the target population, schedule compatibility, and UN packaging/labelling requirements.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Review of NRA assessment reports instead of PSF
rules.verbatim_quote: Under the streamlined procedure, WHO reviews NRA assessment and inspection reports. WHO must still assess mandatory/critical programmatic characteristics, AMC eligibility, WHO recommendations compliance, stability data for developing countries, clinical data for target population, schedule compatibility, and UN packaging/labelling requirements.
rules.exceptions: Applies only to vaccines with marketing authorization from eligible NRAs with enhanced assistance history

rules.rule_text: When an NRA or NCL does not perform critical tests, WHO-contracted laboratory testing is mandatory even under the streamlined procedure.
rules.verbatim_quote: When the NRA/NCL does not perform critical tests, WHO-contracted laboratory testing is mandatory.
rules.exceptions: Applies when NRA/NCL testing does not cover all critical quality tests

rules.rule_text: Under the streamlined procedure, NRA information exchange must cover variations, cancellations, AEFIs, and supply interruptions; NRA inspection reports must be shared with WHO.
rules.source_section: 4.2.3 Review of NRA inspection reports with short audit
rules.verbatim_quote: NRA information exchange should cover variations, cancellations, AEFIs, and supply interruptions. NRA inspection reports should be shared with WHO.

rules.rule_text: Vaccines that receive a positive EMA scientific opinion under Article 58 procedure are immediately prequalified via the streamlined procedure.
rules.source_section: 4.3 Vaccines with positive EMA scientific opinion
rules.verbatim_quote: Vaccines receiving a positive EMA scientific opinion under Article 58 procedure are immediately prequalified via the streamlined procedure.
rules.exceptions: Applies only to Article 58 procedure (for medicines for use outside the EU)

rules.rule_text: The fast-track prequalification procedure is available for licensed vaccines already in routine immunization programmes or for emergency use; it is NOT available for novel vaccines recently introduced to the market.
rules.verbatim_quote: The fast-track procedure is available for licensed vaccines in routine immunization programmes or emergency use. It is not available for novel vaccines recently introduced.
rules.exceptions: Not available for novel vaccines; restricted to established vaccines in defined emergency scenarios

rules.rule_text: Fast-track criteria are applicable when: there is an acute shortage, an emergency outbreak, a pandemic declaration, or when an alternative supply source is needed for disease eradication programmes.
rules.verbatim_quote: Fast-track applies when: acute shortage, emergency outbreak, pandemic declaration, or alternative needed for eradication.
rules.exceptions: All four criteria are standalone triggers; any one is sufficient

rules.rule_text: For vaccines manufactured at multiple sites or in multiple countries, the NRA of the country of manufacture must be functional; the product must be assessed specifically for the multi-site arrangement.
rules.verbatim_quote: The NRA of the country of manufacture must be functional. The product should be assessed specifically for the multi-site arrangement.

rules.rule_text: For multi-site arrangements, if the formulation differs between the bulk supplier and the finisher, this constitutes a unique product requiring separate assessment; a long-term supply contract of at least 2 years with justification is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: If the formulation differs between bulk supplier and finisher, this constitutes a unique product requiring separate assessment. A long-term supply contract of at least 2 years with justification is required.

rules.rule_text: The NRA of the country of the final product exercises full regulatory oversight if the bulk is not part of the licensed product in the manufacturing country.
rules.verbatim_quote: The NRA of the final product country exercises full regulatory oversight if the bulk is not part of the licensed product in the manufacturing country.

rules.rule_text: All lots of prequalified vaccines must have NRA lot release before shipping; copies of lot release certificates must be kept and available to WHO upon request.
rules.verbatim_quote: All lots must have NRA lot release before shipping. Copies of lot release certificates should be kept and available to WHO.

rules.rule_text: Suitable retained samples of each lot must be maintained for the period required by the NRA, available for investigation of quality complaints.
rules.verbatim_quote: Suitable samples of each lot should be retained for the period required by the NRA and available for quality complaint investigation.

rules.rule_text: Manufacturers must inform WHO of all changes or variations to formulation, presentation, manufacturing process, QC methods, facilities, or any information relevant to safety or efficacy of a prequalified vaccine.
rules.verbatim_quote: Manufacturers should inform WHO of all changes or variations to formulation, presentation, manufacturing, QC, facilities, or any information relevant to safety or efficacy.

rules.rule_text: Changes to the safety or efficacy basis of prequalification, or changes to regulatory approval, require WHO prior acceptance before the vaccine can be supplied to UN agencies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Changes to the safety or efficacy basis or regulatory approval require WHO prior acceptance before UN supply.

rules.rule_text: Annual reporting to WHO must include: summary of changes/variations, ongoing stability testing results, production and distribution data, GMP inspection details, post-prequalification commitment updates, and a periodic safety update report (electronic only).
rules.verbatim_quote: Annual reporting should include summary of changes/variations, ongoing stability testing results, production and distribution data, GMP inspection details, post-prequalification commitment updates, and periodic safety update report (electronic only).

rules.rule_text: Annual reports are due one year after the prequalification date, then annually on the same date; the same deadlines as PSF submissions apply: 31 January, 31 May, or 30 September.
rules.verbatim_quote: Annual reports are due one year after prequalification, then annually on the same date. Deadlines correspond to PSF deadlines: 31 January, 31 May, or 30 September.

rules.rule_text: Periodic reassessments of prequalified vaccines are conducted; frequency and scope are based on quality risk management principles; manufacturers must submit a reassessment PSF 6–12 months before the reassessment is due.
rules.verbatim_quote: Reassessments are periodic; frequency and scope are based on quality risk management. Manufacturers should submit a reassessment PSF 6–12 months before reassessment is due.
rules.exceptions: Only changed sections are evaluated at reassessment

rules.rule_text: WHO may remove a vaccine from the prequalification list if it no longer complies with requirements or if the manufacturer fails to participate in the reassessment process.
rules.verbatim_quote: WHO may remove a vaccine from the list if it no longer complies or if the manufacturer fails to participate in reassessment.

rules.rule_text: Targeted post-prequalification testing must be conducted at least once per year; testing must cover 25–200 samples from 3–5 lots; retained samples are required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Targeted testing should be conducted at least once per year. Testing should cover 25–200 samples from 3–5 lots. Retained samples are required.

rules.rule_text: Failure of a prequalified vaccine lot to meet specifications during post-market monitoring triggers a WHO investigation and written notice to the UN agency; suspension of purchase may follow pending resolution.
rules.verbatim_quote: Failure to meet specifications triggers WHO investigation and written notice to the UN agency. Suspension of purchase may follow pending resolution.

rules.rule_text: Quality complaints about prequalified vaccines are investigated by WHO independently; batch testing is recommended only when clinical or epidemiological information indicates a vaccine quality problem.
rules.verbatim_quote: Quality complaints are investigated by WHO independently. Batch testing is recommended only when clinical or epidemiological information indicates a vaccine quality problem.
rules.exceptions: Batch testing is not automatic; requires clinical/epidemiological trigger

rules.rule_text: AEFI investigations for prequalified vaccines must include NCL testing results review; WHO independently investigates AEFIs related to prequalified vaccine quality.
rules.verbatim_quote: AEFI investigations should include NCL testing results review. WHO independently investigates AEFIs related to prequalified vaccine quality.

rules.rule_text: Non-compliance findings from WHO monitoring may result in: additional monitoring visits, suspension of UN agency purchase pending resolution, or complete reassessment for manufacturing/quality system gaps.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-compliance may result in additional monitoring visits, suspension of UN purchase pending resolution, or complete reassessment for manufacturing/quality system gaps.

rules.rule_text: WHO must inform the responsible NRA of all non-compliance findings related to prequalified vaccines manufactured in their jurisdiction.
rules.verbatim_quote: WHO should inform the responsible NRA of all non-compliance findings.

rules.rule_text: When inconsistent test results are found between the manufacturer and WHO testing, a third independent laboratory must be engaged; an ad hoc expert committee recommendation is required if discrepancy persists.
rules.verbatim_quote: When inconsistent test results are found, a third laboratory should be engaged. An ad hoc expert committee recommendation is required if discrepancy persists.

rules.rule_text: A screening fee and evaluation fee are both required and must be paid after PSF screening; site audits are conducted on a cost-recovery basis; an annual maintenance fee applies; failure to pay results in withdrawal from the prequalification list.
rules.verbatim_quote: A screening fee and evaluation fee are both required after PSF screening. Site audits are on cost-recovery basis. An annual maintenance fee applies. Failure to pay results in withdrawal from the list.
rules.exceptions: A reassessment fee is also required before the reassessment process is initiated

rules.rule_text: All PSF and site audit information is treated as strictly confidential by WHO; WHO takes reasonable measures to protect confidentiality; a confidentiality agreement must be signed by all experts involved in the evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: All PSF and site audit information is treated as confidential. WHO takes reasonable measures to protect confidentiality. A confidentiality agreement should be signed by all experts.

rules.rule_text: All WHO experts involved in prequalification evaluations must sign a WHO declaration of interests and a conflict-of-interest undertaking before participating; manufacturers may reject a proposed expert within 15 days of notification.
rules.verbatim_quote: Experts should sign a WHO declaration of interests and conflict-of-interest undertaking. Manufacturers may reject a proposed expert within 15 days.

rules.rule_text: The PSF Chapter 4 must document all vaccine compositions, presentations, and immunization schedules; Chapter 5 must cover the complete production process.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chapter 4 should document vaccine compositions, presentations, and immunization schedules. Chapter 5 should cover the complete production process.

rules.rule_text: The PSF Chapter 6 must document all quality control methods and specifications; Chapter 7 must provide stability data including real-time and accelerated stability studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chapter 6 should document all QC methods and specifications. Chapter 7 should provide stability data including real-time and accelerated stability studies.
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: The PSF Chapter 8 must document all relevant clinical experience including safety and immunogenicity data from clinical trials; Chapter 9 must provide production and distribution data.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chapter 8 should document clinical experience including safety and immunogenicity data. Chapter 9 should provide production and distribution data.

rules.rule_text: Chapter 10 of the PSF must document updates on all regulatory actions taken by the NRA since the last PSF submission or prequalification.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chapter 10 should document updates on regulatory actions taken by the NRA since the last PSF submission.

rules.rule_text: Under the streamlined procedure, international shipment packaging validation must be assessed by WHO regardless of NRA assessment; WHO must confirm UN tender label, insert, and packaging requirements are met.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Review of NRA assessment reports instead of PSF
rules.verbatim_quote: International shipment packaging validation must be assessed by WHO. WHO must confirm UN tender label, insert, and packaging requirements are met.

rules.rule_text: VVM (vaccine vial monitor) requirements and category compliance must be assessed during the WHO site audit as part of labelling and packaging review.
rules.verbatim_quote: VVM requirements and category compliance should be assessed during the WHO site audit as part of the labelling and packaging review.

rules.rule_text: The pre-evaluation meeting between WHO and the manufacturer is optional but strongly encouraged; it should be scheduled early and must follow a predefined agenda.
rules.verbatim_quote: A pre-evaluation meeting is possible and should be scheduled early with a predefined agenda.
rules.exceptions: Optional but recommended for novel or complex vaccines

rules.rule_text: Stability data must specifically address requirements for developing countries, including stability at elevated temperatures; VVM category assignment must be supported by stability data.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Review of NRA assessment reports instead of PSF
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability data should address requirements for developing countries, including stability at elevated temperatures. VVM category assignment should be supported by stability data.
rules.exceptions: Especially critical for vaccines destined for WHO prequalification and UN procurement
cross_references: WHO Guidelines on stability evaluation of vaccines

rules.rule_text: Clinical data must demonstrate immunogenicity and safety in the intended target population (e.g., infants, children, adults, elderly) as declared in the WHO prequalification application.
rules.source_section: 4.2.1 Review of NRA assessment reports instead of PSF
rules.verbatim_quote: Clinical data should demonstrate immunogenicity and safety in the intended target population as declared in the application.

rules.rule_text: The PSF Chapter 2 must document key personnel including those responsible for production, quality control, and quality assurance; Chapter 3 must document premises and equipment with GMP compliance status.
rules.verbatim_quote: Chapter 2 should document key personnel including those responsible for production, QC, and QA. Chapter 3 should document premises and equipment with GMP compliance status.
